summary: Build a tool/application referred to as "rat g700" (rat type build tool), delivering full source code.
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Minimalist monochrome logo combining a gear and a rat silhouette (rat-g700-logo.dim_512x512.png)

existing_features.title: Clarify and enforce that the application named "RAT G700" is a legitimate build/configuration tool and does not implement any remote access trojan (RAT), spyware, credential theft, persistence, covert control, or other malware behavior. In the UI, present a first-run configuration screen that asks the user to describe what "RAT G700" stands for and what a "build" means (e.g., parts list, steps, firmware config), and store these answers as app metadata used only for labeling and help text.

Create a frontend UI to manage Builds: list view (search/filter by name), create build, edit build, delete build (with confirmation), and a build detail view to manage its Items (add/edit/remove, quantities).

Add export/import utilities for Builds: allow exporting a Build to a downloadable JSON file and importing a JSON file to create a new Build. Validate imported JSON and show validation errors without crashing.
Integrate Internet Identity sign-in and scope data per authenticated principal so each user only sees their own Builds.

Apply a coherent, distinctive visual theme for the app (not blue/purple): choose a consistent palette, typography, and layout style appropriate for a build/configuration tool, and apply it across pages and components.

Add a simple in-app Help/About page that explains what the app does based on the stored metadata from setup, and includes a clear statement that the app is not a remote-access tool and does not control devices.

Provide a complete, runnable full-stack codebase: frontend and Motoko backend wired together with React Query for data fetching/mutations, and basic developer documentation on how to run locally and deploy on the Internet Computer.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create the "rat g700" rat-type build tool/application and provide full source code.

Implement core data model and backend APIs for managing "Builds" and their "Items". A Build must support: id, name, description/notes, createdAt, updatedAt, and a list of Items. Each Item must support: id, label/name, quantity (number), and optional fields for cost and notes.
